mandates.mandate: Runs BEFORE self-critique so rationalizations are caught before confidence scoring.
The agent cannot rationalize skipping the anti-rationalization check itself.
Adding new excuses to a persona's table is encouraged as project-specific patterns emerge.
If no agent persona is loaded (orchestrator-owned workflow), use the built-in common excuse table.

excuse-rebuttal.rebuttal: Document the edge case. Let PM triage priority. Rare does not mean ignorable.
Check against acceptance criteria. If criteria are met, it IS good enough. If not, it is not.
If the issue is known now, track it now. Create a tech-debt story or note it in the current story.
Verify against the PRD traces_to chain. If truly out of scope, note it for the PM. If in scope, do the work.

excuse-rebuttal.excuse: The user probably doesn't care about this
excuse-rebuttal.rebuttal: The user defined acceptance criteria. Check them. Do not assume what the user cares about.
Estimate the actual effort. If genuinely large, flag as a scope concern rather than silently cutting.

step.title: Scan draft for skip/defer/simplify decisions
step.action: Read the draft output about to be persisted.
Identify any decisions that skip, defer, simplify, downgrade, or omit declared scope items.
If no such decisions found: return PASS.

step.title: Load excuse table
step.action: If agent persona has anti-rationalization block: use persona table PLUS built-in table.
If no persona loaded: use only the built-in table.

step.title: Check each decision against the table
step.action: For each skip/defer/simplify decision: compare against each excuse pattern (semantic match).
If match found: flag the rationalization, apply the rebuttal, revise the section.
If no match: leave unchanged, annotate as reviewed.

step.title: Return result
step.action: If rationalizations caught and revised: return REVISED with count and summary.
